sales.title:

Diocletian (AD 284-305). BI antoninianus (22mm, 3.66 gm, 1h). NGC Choice MS 5/5 - 5/5, Silvering. Siscia, 1st officina, AD 289-290. IMP C C VAL DIOCLETIANVS P F AVG, radiate, cuirassed bust o
sales.description:
Ancients
Diocletian (AD 284-305). BI antoninianus (22mm, 3.66 gm, 1h). NGC Choice MS 5/5 - 5/5, Silvering. Siscia, 1st officina, AD 289-290. IMP C C VAL DIOCLETIANVS P F AVG, radiate, cuirassed bust of Diocletian right, seen from front / CONSERVATOR-AVGG, emperor standing right (on left), Jupiter standing facing, head left (on right), each sacrificing at altar between them from patera in outstretched right hand, grounded scepter in left; XXIA•I• in exergue. RIC V.II 263.

Ex Heritage Auctions,...

sales.title:
Heritage World Coin Auctions - Showcase Auction 61605, Lot 23221 — Ancients
Diocletian (AD 284-305). BI antoninianus (23mm, 3.43 gm, 1h). NGC Choice MS 5/5 - 4/5, Silvering, flan flaw. Lugdunum, 2nd officina, ca. AD 285. IMP C C VAL DIOCLETIANVS P F AVF, radiate, dra
sales.description:
Ancients
Diocletian (AD 284-305). BI antoninianus (23mm, 3.43 gm, 1h). NGC Choice MS 5/5 - 4/5, Silvering, flan flaw. Lugdunum, 2nd officina, ca. AD 285. IMP C C VAL DIOCLETIANVS P F AVF, radiate, draped, and cuirassed bust of Diocletian right / IOVI CONSERVAT AVG, Jupiter standing facing, head left, thunderbolt in outstretched right hand, grounded scepter in left; B in inner left field. RIC V.II 41.

When Diocletian came into power, he realized that the empire was too large to be run by one p...
